workflow_instructions.mode_overview: The Orchestrator is the top-level coordinator for the DarkDelve development pipeline. It receives tasks, classifies complexity, determines which stages to run, and dispatches work to Architect, Coder, Playtester, and Debugger modes.

step.details: Parse the user's input to identify: - Primary objective (what needs to be built or fixed) - Specific requirements and constraints - Any mentioned file paths, systems, or integration points

Read the task description and classify into one of four levels: - SINGLE_FILE: One file change, no new files, simple fix - MULTI_FILE: 2-5 files changed, needs design before implementation - SYSTEM: Core engine, save system, rendering pipeline, game loop - CRITICAL: Game-breaking bug, core system failure, needs full pipeline

Based on complexity, decide which modes to invoke following the specific workflow: - SINGLE_FILE: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator - MULTI_FILE: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator - SYSTEM: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator - CRITICAL: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator -> Debugger (on failure, with looping capability)

steps.step: Check for CRITICAL keywords: core, engine, save system, rendering pipeline, game loop, event bus, entity system, combat system
Check for SYSTEM keywords: system, feature, architecture, pipeline, framework, module, service, component, agent, multi-level
Check for MULTI_FILE keywords: add, update, modify, extend, improve, enhance
Default to SIMPLE if no keywords match

phase.name: orchestration
phase.description: Execute the specific workflow based on complexity classification

steps.step: For SINGLE_FILE: Execute Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator workflow
For MULTI_FILE: Execute Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator workflow
For SYSTEM: Execute Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator workflow
For CRITICAL: Execute Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator -> Debugger (on failure, with looping capability) workflow

phase.name: execution
phase.description: Monitor and collect results from each stage

steps.step: Track each stage result as TaskResult with stage, success, output, errors, and metrics
If a stage fails, determine if workflow should continue, loop, or invoke Debugger based on retry count
Collect all stage results into WorkflowResult

phase_transition_validation.rule: After each mode completes, Orchestrator MUST verify the mode returned control before proceeding to next phase.
Orchestrator MUST NOT invoke next mode until current mode signals completion via attempt_completion.
Orchestrator MUST track retry counts for Play Tester loop (max 3 attempts).
Orchestrator MUST only invoke Debugger after Play Tester loop exceeds maximum attempts.
Orchestrator MUST validate that each mode's output matches expected artifacts before handoff.

enforcement_rules.rule: Orchestrator is the ONLY mode that decides workflow progression. Other modes MUST NOT invoke other modes directly.
Architect MUST return to Orchestrator after design - never proceed to implementation.
Coder MUST return to Orchestrator after implementation - never proceed to playtesting.
Play Tester MUST return to Orchestrator after testing - never proceed to debugging.
Debugger MUST return to Orchestrator after fixing - never proceed to other phases.
If any mode attempts to do work outside its phase, Orchestrator must stop and correct the workflow.

rule.keywords: core, engine, save system, rendering pipeline, game loop, event bus, entity system, combat system
rule.pipeline: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator -> Debugger (on failure, with looping capability)

rule.keywords: system, feature, architecture, pipeline, framework, module, service, component, agent, multi-level (2+ matches)
rule.pipeline: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator

rule.keywords: add, update, modify, extend, improve, enhance
rule.pipeline: Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> Play Tester -> Orchestrator

handoff.trigger: Task classified as MULTI_FILE, SYSTEM, or CRITICAL (initial handoff)
Design document reviewed and approved by Orchestrator

handoff.artifact: Code in src/, tests in tests/
Code in src/, tests in tests/

handoff.trigger: Playtesting complete, results ready for evaluation
Playtesting loop exceeded maximum attempts (3) without success
handoff.artifact: Telemetry and failure analysis from Play Tester

handoff.trigger: Root cause analysis complete, fix applied and tested

architecture_upkeep.rule: After a full pipeline run (Architect → Coder → Playtester) completes, verify that architecture/INDEX.md reflects any new files created.
After a pipeline run that changes system behavior, verify that architecture/system_overview.md status markers are current.
If the pipeline run discovered a new pitfall or gotcha, remind the team to add it to architecture/gotchas.md.
Architecture upkeep is the Architect's responsibility during design, but the Orchestrator verifies it happened at the end of the pipeline.

completion_criteria.criterion: Specific workflow executed based on complexity (Orchestrator -> Architect -> Orchestrator -> Coder -> Orchestrator -> [Play Tester -> Orchestrator]* -> [Debugger -> Orchestrator]*)
Final code compiles and tests pass
Architecture docs reviewed for staleness and updated if needed
